metadata.title: States PCS: Punjab GK Questions for PPSC Examinations

questions.question: What was the sex ratio in Punjab as per the 2011 Census?
questions.explanation: According to 2011 Census, Punjab’s sex ratio was 895, up from 876 in 2001, still among lowest in India. Haryana and Delhi also report low ratios. National average in 2011 was 943. Punjab has implemented schemes to improve the gender balance post-2011.

questions.question: Which Punjab district has the highest child sex ratio?
questions.explanation: SBS Nagar recorded the highest child sex ratio (885 girls per 1000 boys) among Punjab districts, as per Census 2011 data. Other major districts like Patiala, Amritsar, and Ludhiana have lower child sex ratio figures compared to SBS Nagar.

questions.question: Fatehgarh Sahib in Punjab was a medieval fortress town and important for?
questions.explanation: Fatehgarh Sahib was a key Mughal administrative division, later named after Sahibzada Fateh Singh, son of Guru Gobind Singh Ji. It served as a medieval fortress town of political and historical importance in Punjab.

questions.question: Which Delhi Sultan’s mother was a Bhatti Rajput princess from Abohar, Punjab?
questions.explanation: Firoz Shah Tughlaq’s mother was a Bhatti Rajput princess from Abohar, Punjab. He founded cities like Firozpur, Hisar, and Fatehabad, and developed extensive irrigation systems, contributing substantially to the prosperity of Punjab during his reign as Delhi Sultan (1351-1388).

questions.question: Who died in the 1757 Battle of Amritsar fighting Afghan forces?
questions.explanation: Baba Deep Singh died in 1757 at Amritsar while fighting Ahmad Shah Durrani’s Afghan troops, defending the Golden Temple. He is revered for his valour and martyrdom; a memorial stands at the site of his last battle in Amritsar, Punjab.

questions.question: Who commanded Sikh forces at the 1702 Battle of Basoli?
questions.explanation: Guru Gobind Singh led the Sikhs in the 1702 Battle of Basoli, defeating combined Mughal and Hill Raja forces led by Raja Ajmer Chand. The battle followed the Sikh victory at Nirmohgarh. Guru Gobind Singh’s victory forced the enemy retreat and led to a truce with Raja Ajmer Chand.

questions.question: Who led Sikh forces in the 1763 Battle of Sialkot?
questions.explanation: Charat Singh led Sikh forces at Sialkot in 1763, defeating Durrani commander Jahan Khan. The Sikhs captured the Afghan camp, liberated prisoners, and forced Afghan retreat, avenging earlier Afghan atrocities. This victory was a key moment in Sikh resistance against the Durrani Empire.

questions.question: Where was Guru Ram Das Ji born in 1534?
questions.explanation: Guru Ram Das Ji, the fourth Sikh Guru, was born in 1534 at Chuna Mandi, Lahore, now in Pakistan.

questions.question: Guru Gobind Singh’s compositions are compiled in which holy book?
questions.explanation: Dasam Granth contains Guru Gobind Singh’s writings. It is distinct from Adi Granth, finalized by Guru Gobind Singh, who declared Guru Granth Sahib the perpetual Sikh Guru after his death.

questions.question: Where was the Khalsa order founded by Guru Gobind Singh Ji in 1699?

options.label: A
options.text: Takht Sri Keshgarh Sahib, Anandpur Sahib

options.label: B
options.text: Takht Sri Damdama Sahib, Talwandi Sabo

answer.label: A
answer.text: Takht Sri Keshgarh Sahib, Anandpur Sahib

questions.explanation: Takht Sri Keshgarh Sahib, Anandpur Sahib, Punjab, is where Guru Gobind Singh Ji founded the Khalsa order in 1699. The site preserves Sikh relics and is among Sikhism’s five takhts. Other options are significant Sikh shrines but not connected to the founding of the Khalsa.
